excluded_segments.description: Fenced or inline code — implementation detail, not author voice
Raw URL tokens; link text is still scored

ai_like.name: Repetitive phrasing / same sentence shapes
ai_like.definition: Multiple paragraphs reuse the same opener, transition, or syntactic pattern.
ai_like.ai_example: As noted earlier, planning is essential. Similarly, execution matters. As noted earlier, review is key.
ai_like.human_example: Planning matters. Execution matters more — and most teams forget the third step: actually shipping.
ai_like.fix_tip: Vary sentence openers; cut transitional crutches.

ai_like.name: Mechanical transitions
ai_like.definition: Phrases like Firstly / Secondly / Lastly or In conclusion used as filler rather than real structure.
ai_like.ai_example: Firstly, plan. Secondly, execute. Lastly, review.
ai_like.human_example: Plan first. Then ship something rough. Review only after it is live.
ai_like.fix_tip: Remove numbered connectors; let one idea lead to the next in plain language.

ai_like.name: Generic blog openers
ai_like.definition: Vague context-setting that could apply to any post in the category.
ai_like.ai_example: In today’s fast-paced world, content is more important than ever.
ai_like.human_example: We deleted half our blog posts last quarter — traffic went up.
ai_like.fix_tip: Open with a specific image, number, or moment; delete throat-clearing.

ai_like.name: Hedging / laundry-list qualifiers
ai_like.definition: Strings of mitigations (may, might, arguably) that blur any claim.
ai_like.ai_example: It might arguably be suggested that performance could potentially improve.
ai_like.human_example: This cut p95 latency by 38% on our worst tenant.
ai_like.fix_tip: Make one falsifiable claim per paragraph.

ai_like.name: Outline pasted as prose
ai_like.definition: Dense bullets where each item has the same grammatical shape — reads like a slide deck.
ai_like.ai_example:
- Benefit one…
- Benefit two…
- Benefit three…
ai_like.human_example: Two wins mattered — fewer timeouts, and support stopped paging us nightly.
ai_like.fix_tip: Keep bullets sparse; turn the strongest items into narrative paragraphs.

ai_like.name: Suspiciously uniform paragraphs
ai_like.definition: Paragraphs are similar length with parallel closing rhythms — typical of templated generation.
ai_like.ai_example: Three paragraphs each ending with "…which drives better outcomes."
ai_like.human_example: Mixed lengths; one paragraph is a single blunt sentence.
ai_like.fix_tip: Break rhythm on purpose — short punchy line after a dense paragraph.

ai_like.name: Stock AI template phrases
ai_like.definition: Phrases like "delve into", "landscape", "robust", "leverage", "unlock value" without specifics.
ai_like.ai_example: Let’s delve into the robust landscape of modern tooling.
ai_like.human_example: We replaced Cron with a tiny worker — here is what broke first.
ai_like.fix_tip: Swap abstract nouns for verbs you actually performed.

ai_like.name: Abstraction without anchoring examples
ai_like.definition: Claims stay at principle level — no numbers, tools, dates, or places readers can picture.
ai_like.ai_example: Teams should align stakeholders across the initiative lifecycle.
ai_like.human_example: We rolled Postgres 15 on a Tuesday; rollback script lived in /ops/rollback.sh.
ai_like.fix_tip: Add one concrete anchor — version, metric, filename, or date.

human_like.name: First-person experience with stakes
human_like.definition: First-person story attached to a consequence — failure, trade-off, or uncomfortable choice.
human_like.ai_example: Teams should consider trade-offs before deploying.
human_like.human_example: We deployed on a Friday and our pager went off at 2 a.m. Never again.
human_like.fix_tip: Keep the honest anecdote — specificity lowers AI-likeness scores.

human_like.name: Specific proper nouns and numbers
human_like.definition: Named tools, versions, metrics, customers redacted but concrete ("tenant
human_like.human_example: Datadog fired before users noticed — p95 dropped from 4.2s to 890ms.
human_like.fix_tip: Swap vague benefits for one measurable delta.

human_like.name: Opinionated voice / mild bias
human_like.definition: Takes a side; uses judgment words a neutral encyclopedia would avoid.
human_like.human_example: I would ship the boring queue before the clever cache — every time.
human_like.fix_tip: State what you would do, not what "one might consider".

human_like.name: Conversational asides
human_like.definition: Parenthetical humor, self-interruption, informal punctuation — marks human drafting rhythm.
human_like.human_example: Reliability first — yes, even before the roadmap theme (sorry, PMs).
human_like.fix_tip: One aside per section is enough; keep it tight.

human_like.name: Imperfect rhythm / fragment sentences
human_like.definition: Deliberate fragments, em-dash thought breaks, uneven sentence length — unlike polished templates.
human_like.ai_example: It is important to note that implementation requires careful planning.
human_like.human_example: Careful planning — honestly? — matters less than starting messy.
human_like.fix_tip: Allow one fragment or short sentence for emphasis.

human_like.name: Domain jargon used precisely
human_like.definition: Terms of art appear in context that proves the author works in the domain — not buzzword salad.
human_like.human_example: We pinned libc at 2.35 — glibc DNS bug bit us last summer.
human_like.fix_tip: Define jargon by example, not by synonym chaining.
